    10→  Spec refs: wrapper-contract (dev warnings section)

    15→  Spec refs: fix-button items 1, 2, 6

    17→  Done: iteration 2. Added useRef/useEffect imports, isDev import. Removed module-level let flags and isDevBuild const. Added hasWarnedSpan, hasWarnedMissingLabel, hasWarnedOnChange useRef guards. All refs placed before early return to satisfy rules of hooks. typecheck and jest failures are pre-existing (no new errors introduced).
    18→
    19→- [x] 3. Fix Button.tsx — destructive normalisation + span branch docs + delete dead .jsx
    20→  Context: Move `destructive → danger` theme normalisation before the `changeTag === 'span'` branch split (currently line 202 only normalises inside span branch, but the TUI path at line 253 passes `resolvedTheme` which could be `'destructive'`). Add fragility comment to span branch explaining it manually replicates TUI classes. Delete `Button.jsx` if it exists (superseded by `.tsx`).
    21→  Spec refs: fix-button items 3, 4, 5
    22→  Done: iteration 3. Split `resolvedTheme` into `rawTheme`+normalised `resolvedTheme` (destructive→danger) before branch split. Removed duplicate `themeClass` const in span branch; span now uses `resolvedTheme` directly. Replaced brief inline comment with `@fragile` JSDoc block. Button.jsx deletion skipped (doesn't exist).
    23→
    24→- [x] 4. Fix Text.tsx — TypeScript annotations (`FieldsTextProps` interface)
    25→  Context: `TextField` component is untyped (`props => {`). Add `FieldsTextProps` interface covering all used props: `dynamic`, `inputMask`, `prefix`, `suffix`, `value`, `onChange`, `name`, `placeholder`, `readOnly`, `isDisabled`, `isRequired`, `isInvalid`, `error`, `label`, `labelVisuallyHidden`, `description`, `descriptionVisuallyHidden`, `className`, `inputClassName`, `size`, `type`. Type the component signature.
    26→  Spec refs: fix-text item 1
    27→  Done: iteration 4. Added `FieldsTextProps` interface; imported `SizeStandard`/`TextInputType` from `@tangible/ui` for accurate types. Typed `useRef` as `HTMLInputElement | null`. Cast `useTextField` args as `any` (to be cleaned up in task 6). No new typecheck errors introduced.
    28→
    29→- [x] 5. Fix Text.tsx — onChange mount-fire bug (mountedRef guard)
    30→  Context: `useEffect(() => { if (props.onChange) props.onChange(value) }, [value])` fires on mount, sending the initial value to the store immediately. Add a `mountedRef` guard: skip the first invocation, only fire onChange on subsequent value changes.
    31→  Spec refs: fix-text item 2
    32→  Dependencies: Task 4
    33→  Done: iteration 5. Added `mountedRef = useRef(false)`; effect sets it true on first run and returns early, so onChange only fires on subsequent value changes.
    34→
    35→- [x] 6. Fix Text.tsx — remove useTextField from TUI path + add forwardRef
    36→  Context: `useTextField(props, ref)` is called unconditionally but its return values (`labelProps`, `inputProps`, `descriptionProps`) are only used in the dynamic path. Move the call inside the `if (hasDynamic)` block. Add `forwardRef<HTMLInputElement>` to the component — TUI TextInput forwards its ref to the `<input>` element.
    37→  Spec refs: fix-text items 3, 4
    38→  Dependencies: Task 4
    39→  Done: iteration 6. Extracted `DynamicTextField` internal sub-component that owns `useTextField`, its own state, and the dynamic render path. Main `TextField` wrapped with `forwardRef<HTMLInputElement>`; forwarded ref passed to `TuiTextInput` via callback ref (also handles inputMaskRef). querySelector in ref callback preserved for task 7.
    40→
    41→- [ ] 7. Fix Text.tsx — remove querySelector, fix inputMaskRef via TUI ref
    42→  Context: Line 113 uses `node.querySelector?.('input') ?? node` to get the input element for masking. TUI TextInput ref IS the `<input>` element, so `querySelector` is unnecessary. Simplify: `inputMaskRef.current = node`. Add comment explaining `Field.HelperText` className approach (using `tui-visually-hidden` class because Field.HelperText doesn't expose a `hidden` prop).
    43→  Spec refs: fix-text items 5, 6
    44→  Dependencies: Task 6
    45→
    46→- [ ] 8. Add/update Jest tests for TUI render paths (Button mapped layouts, Text non-dynamic)
    47→  Context: Existing tests only cover legacy Button path and dynamic Text path. Add tests for: (a) Button with mapped layout (e.g., `layout="primary"`) renders TUI Button, (b) Button with unmapped layout falls back to LegacyButton, (c) Text without `dynamic` prop renders TUI TextInput, (d) Text onChange doesn't fire on mount. Place in `tests/jest/cases/components/`.
    48→  Spec refs: quality gate
    49→  Dependencies: Tasks 2, 3, 5, 6, 7
    50→
    51→## Discoveries
    52→
    53→- **Control.tsx has the same onChange-on-mount bug** as Text.tsx (useEffect fires on mount). Systemic issue affecting all fields. Don't fix here — broader change needed.
    54→- **`strict: false` in tsconfig** — typecheck catches errors but won't enforce strict nulls. Good enough for migration phase.
    55→- **Text tests only cover dynamic/CodeMirror path** — no existing coverage for the TUI TextInput render path.
    56→- **Button tests only cover legacy path** — mapped layouts that go through TUI have no test coverage.
    57→- **Button.jsx does NOT exist** — only Button.tsx and LegacyButton.tsx. Task 3 can skip the deletion step.

# Implementation Plan

Generated: 2026-03-05
Last updated: 2026-03-05T19:49:37Z (iteration 1)

## Tasks

- [x] 1. Create shared `isDev()` utility at `assets/src/utils/is-dev.ts`
  Context: Shared utility for dev-only warnings. Pattern: `export const isDev = (): boolean => ...` checking `import.meta.env?.DEV` or `process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'`. See `.harness/specs/wrapper-contract.md` for usage pattern.
  Spec refs: wrapper-contract (dev warnings section)
  Done: iteration 1. Created `assets/src/utils/is-dev.ts`. Used `unknown` cast to avoid ImportMeta type mismatch — tsconfig has no vite/import.meta types defined.

- [x] 2. Fix Button.tsx — warning guards + isDev import
  Context: Replace module-level `let hasWarnedAbout*` flags with `useRef` guards inside the component. Replace hand-rolled `isDevBuild` const with `isDev()` import. Add dev warning when `onChange` prop is passed (currently silently destructured to `_onChange`).
  Spec refs: fix-button items 1, 2, 6
  Dependencies: Task 1
  Done: iteration 2. Added useRef/useEffect imports, isDev import. Removed module-level let flags and isDevBuild const. Added hasWarnedSpan, hasWarnedMissingLabel, hasWarnedOnChange useRef guards. All refs placed before early return to satisfy rules of hooks. typecheck and jest failures are pre-existing (no new errors introduced).

- [x] 3. Fix Button.tsx — destructive normalisation + span branch docs + delete dead .jsx
  Context: Move `destructive → danger` theme normalisation before the `changeTag === 'span'` branch split (currently line 202 only normalises inside span branch, but the TUI path at line 253 passes `resolvedTheme` which could be `'destructive'`). Add fragility comment to span branch explaining it manually replicates TUI classes. Delete `Button.jsx` if it exists (superseded by `.tsx`).
  Spec refs: fix-button items 3, 4, 5
  Done: iteration 3. Split `resolvedTheme` into `rawTheme`+normalised `resolvedTheme` (destructive→danger) before branch split. Removed duplicate `themeClass` const in span branch; span now uses `resolvedTheme` directly. Replaced brief inline comment with `@fragile` JSDoc block. Button.jsx deletion skipped (doesn't exist).

- [x] 4. Fix Text.tsx — TypeScript annotations (`FieldsTextProps` interface)
  Context: `TextField` component is untyped (`props => {`). Add `FieldsTextProps` interface covering all used props: `dynamic`, `inputMask`, `prefix`, `suffix`, `value`, `onChange`, `name`, `placeholder`, `readOnly`, `isDisabled`, `isRequired`, `isInvalid`, `error`, `label`, `labelVisuallyHidden`, `description`, `descriptionVisuallyHidden`, `className`, `inputClassName`, `size`, `type`. Type the component signature.
  Spec refs: fix-text item 1
  Done: iteration 4. Added `FieldsTextProps` interface; imported `SizeStandard`/`TextInputType` from `@tangible/ui` for accurate types. Typed `useRef` as `HTMLInputElement | null`. Cast `useTextField` args as `any` (to be cleaned up in task 6). No new typecheck errors introduced.

- [x] 5. Fix Text.tsx — onChange mount-fire bug (mountedRef guard)
  Context: `useEffect(() => { if (props.onChange) props.onChange(value) }, [value])` fires on mount, sending the initial value to the store immediately. Add a `mountedRef` guard: skip the first invocation, only fire onChange on subsequent value changes.
  Spec refs: fix-text item 2
  Dependencies: Task 4
  Done: iteration 5. Added `mountedRef = useRef(false)`; effect sets it true on first run and returns early, so onChange only fires on subsequent value changes.

- [x] 6. Fix Text.tsx — remove useTextField from TUI path + add forwardRef
  Context: `useTextField(props, ref)` is called unconditionally but its return values (`labelProps`, `inputProps`, `descriptionProps`) are only used in the dynamic path. Move the call inside the `if (hasDynamic)` block. Add `forwardRef<HTMLInputElement>` to the component — TUI TextInput forwards its ref to the `<input>` element.
  Spec refs: fix-text items 3, 4
  Dependencies: Task 4
  Done: iteration 6. Extracted `DynamicTextField` internal sub-component that owns `useTextField`, its own state, and the dynamic render path. Main `TextField` wrapped with `forwardRef<HTMLInputElement>`; forwarded ref passed to `TuiTextInput` via callback ref (also handles inputMaskRef). querySelector in ref callback preserved for task 7.

- [ ] 7. Fix Text.tsx — remove querySelector, fix inputMaskRef via TUI ref
  Context: Line 113 uses `node.querySelector?.('input') ?? node` to get the input element for masking. TUI TextInput ref IS the `<input>` element, so `querySelector` is unnecessary. Simplify: `inputMaskRef.current = node`. Add comment explaining `Field.HelperText` className approach (using `tui-visually-hidden` class because Field.HelperText doesn't expose a `hidden` prop).
  Spec refs: fix-text items 5, 6
  Dependencies: Task 6

- [ ] 8. Add/update Jest tests for TUI render paths (Button mapped layouts, Text non-dynamic)
  Context: Existing tests only cover legacy Button path and dynamic Text path. Add tests for: (a) Button with mapped layout (e.g., `layout="primary"`) renders TUI Button, (b) Button with unmapped layout falls back to LegacyButton, (c) Text without `dynamic` prop renders TUI TextInput, (d) Text onChange doesn't fire on mount. Place in `tests/jest/cases/components/`.
  Spec refs: quality gate
  Dependencies: Tasks 2, 3, 5, 6, 7

## Discoveries

- **Control.tsx has the same onChange-on-mount bug** as Text.tsx (useEffect fires on mount). Systemic issue affecting all fields. Don't fix here — broader change needed.
- **`strict: false` in tsconfig** — typecheck catches errors but won't enforce strict nulls. Good enough for migration phase.
- **Text tests only cover dynamic/CodeMirror path** — no existing coverage for the TUI TextInput render path.
- **Button tests only cover legacy path** — mapped layouts that go through TUI have no test coverage.
- **Button.jsx does NOT exist** — only Button.tsx and LegacyButton.tsx. Task 3 can skip the deletion step.

# Fields TUI Migration — Progress Log

## Iteration 6 — 2026-03-05T20:01:35Z
Task: Fix Text.tsx — remove useTextField from TUI path + add forwardRef
Result: pass
Files changed: assets/src/components/field/text/Text.tsx
Commit: cd38bb9
Notes: Created internal `DynamicTextField` component to own `useTextField` + dynamic render path. Main `TextField` uses `forwardRef<HTMLInputElement>`; forwarded ref passed to `TuiTextInput` via callback ref that also handles `inputMaskRef`. The `querySelector` hack in the callback ref is preserved — task 7 will remove it. Typecheck: no new errors in Text.tsx. Jest: 33 pre-existing failures (Babel/Jest config), unchanged.

## Iteration 5 — 2026-03-05T20:00:34Z
Task: Fix Text.tsx — onChange mount-fire bug (mountedRef guard)
Result: pass
Files changed: assets/src/components/field/text/Text.tsx
Commit: 5dd6180
Notes: Added `mountedRef = useRef(false)` adjacent to other refs. Effect sets it to `true` on first invocation and returns early — subsequent value changes call `props.onChange` normally. Typecheck and Jest failures remain pre-existing.

## Iteration 4 — 2026-03-05T19:56:38Z
Task: Fix Text.tsx — TypeScript annotations (`FieldsTextProps` interface)
Result: pass
Files changed: assets/src/components/field/text/Text.tsx
Commit: 9d766e8
Notes: Typed `size`/`type` using `SizeStandard`/`TextInputType` from `@tangible/ui`. `useRef()` typed as `HTMLInputElement | null`. `useTextField` args cast as `any` — the `useTextField` call itself will be moved inside the `hasDynamic` block in task 6, removing this cast. Typecheck and Jest failures remain pre-existing.

## Iteration 3 — 2026-03-05T19:55:25Z
Task: Fix Button.tsx — destructive normalisation + span branch docs + delete dead .jsx
Result: pass
Files changed: assets/src/components/base/button/Button.tsx
Commit: 68c7427
Notes: Normalisation moved before branch split by introducing `rawTheme` intermediate. Span branch `themeClass` removed; uses `resolvedTheme` directly. Fragility JSDoc comment added. Typecheck and Jest failures are pre-existing (same as iterations 1 & 2).

## Iteration 2 — 2026-03-05T19:52:50Z
Task: Fix Button.tsx — warning guards + isDev import
Result: pass
Files changed: assets/src/components/base/button/Button.tsx
Commit: b11d6cb
Notes: Typecheck and Jest tests were pre-existing failures (same as iteration 1). No new errors introduced. useRef hooks placed before the !isMappedLayout early return to satisfy rules of hooks. props.onChange referenced inside useEffect closure (not _onChange) matching spec pattern.

## Iteration 1 — 2026-03-05T19:51:42Z
Task: Create shared `isDev()` utility at `assets/src/utils/is-dev.ts`
Result: pass
Files changed: assets/src/utils/is-dev.ts
Commit: 5f795b5
Notes: All 33 Jest test suites were failing before this change (pre-existing Babel/Jest config issue). TypeScript has no `import.meta.env` types — used `unknown` intermediate cast to silence errors. Next task (Fix Button.tsx) can import from `../../../utils/is-dev`.

## Iteration 1 — 2026-03-05T19:49:37Z
Task: Generate implementation plan
Result: pass
Notes: Created plan with 8 tasks from 3 spec files (wrapper-contract, fix-button, fix-text). Plan confirmed against live codebase — all tasks pending, no changes needed from Iteration 0 plan.

## Iteration 0 — 2026-03-05
Task: Generate implementation plan and harness infrastructure
Result: pass
Notes: Created harness config, build prompt, plan with 8 tasks, and 3 spec files (wrapper-contract, fix-button, fix-text). Validated that Button.tsx and Text.tsx exist on feature/tui-integration branch. No Button.jsx to delete. Fields uses jest:test for unit tests, tsconfig has strict: false.

content.content:
1→import { 2→ useRef, 3→ useEffect, 4→ useState, 5→ lazy, 6→ Suspense, 7→ forwardRef 8→} from 'react' 9→ 10→import { useTextField } from 'react-aria' 11→import { Field, TextInput as TuiTextInput } from '@tangible/ui' 12→import type { SizeStandard, TextInputType } from '@tangible/ui' 13→ 14→import Description from '../../base/field/Description' 15→import Label from '../../base/field/Label' 16→import { useInputMask, matchesMask } from '../../../utils/use-input-mask' 17→ 18→const DynamicEditor = lazy(() => import('../../dynamic/editor/DynamicEditor')) 19→ 20→/** 21→ * Text field with three routing paths: 22→ * 1. dynamic → ProseMirror-based DynamicEditor (lazy-loaded) 23→ * 2. inputMask (no dynamic) → TUI TextInput + useInputMask hook 24→ * 3. plain → TUI TextInput with optional prefix/suffix 25→ * 26→ * @see https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/useTextField.html 27→ */ 28→ 29→export interface FieldsTextProps { 30→ dynamic?: unknown 31→ inputMask?: string 32→ prefix?: string 33→ suffix?: string 34→ value?: string 35→ onChange?: (value: string) => void 36→ name?: string 37→ placeholder?: string 38→ readOnly?: boolean 39→ isDisabled?: boolean 40→ isRequired?: boolean 41→ isInvalid?: boolean 42→ error?: boolean 43→ label?: string 44→ labelVisuallyHidden?: boolean 45→ description?: string 46→ descriptionVisuallyHidden?: boolean 47→ className?: string 48→ inputClassName?: string 49→ size?: SizeStandard 50→ type?: TextInputType 51→} 52→ 53→/** 54→ * Dynamic path sub-component — rendered only when the `dynamic` prop is set. 55→ * Isolated here so `useTextField` (react-aria) is only called in this path. 56→ */ 57→const DynamicTextField = (props: FieldsTextProps) => { 58→ const [value, setValue] = useState(props.value ?? '') 59→ const ref = useRef(null) 60→ const mountedRef = useRef(false) 61→ 62→ const { 63→ labelProps, 64→ inputProps, 65→ descriptionProps, 66→ // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any 67→ } = useTextField(props as any, ref as any) 68→ 69→ useEffect(() => { 70→ if (!mountedRef.current) { 71→ mountedRef.current = true 72→ return 73→ } 74→ if (props.onChange) props.onChange(value) 75→ }, [value]) 76→ 77→ return ( 78→
79→ {props.label && 80→ } 83→ 84→ 96→ 97→ {props.description && 98→ 99→ {props.description} 100→ } 101→
102→ ) 103→} 104→ 105→const TextField = forwardRef((props, ref) => { 106→ const { 107→ dynamic, 108→ inputMask, 109→ prefix, 110→ suffix, 111→ } = props 112→ 113→ const hasDynamic = Boolean(dynamic) 114→ 115→ const [value, setValue] = useState(props.value ?? '') 116→ const inputMaskRef = useRef(null) 117→ const mountedRef = useRef(false) 118→ 119→ useInputMask(inputMaskRef, !hasDynamic ? inputMask : null) 120→ 121→ useEffect(() => { 122→ if (!mountedRef.current) { 123→ mountedRef.current = true 124→ return 125→ } 126→ if (props.onChange) props.onChange(value) 127→ }, [value]) 128→ 129→ /** 130→ * Dynamic path — delegate to DynamicTextField which calls useTextField. 131→ * Note: ref is not forwarded to the ProseMirror editor. 132→ */ 133→ if (hasDynamic) { 134→ return 135→ } 136→ 137→ // Validate initial mask value 138→ const getInitialMaskValue = () => { 139→ if (!inputMask || !value) return value 140→ const stripped = stripAffixes(value, prefix, suffix) 141→ return matchesMask(stripped, inputMask) ? value : '' 142→ } 143→ 144→ /** 145→ * Native path — TUI TextInput (with optional input mask, prefix, suffix) 146→ */ 147→ return ( 148→
149→ 155→ {props.label && 156→ 157→ {props.label} 158→ } 159→ 160→ { 162→ if (typeof ref === 'function') ref(node) 163→ else if (ref) ref.current = node 164→ // For input masking, grab the actual element 165→ if (inputMask && node) { 166→ inputMaskRef.current = node.querySelector?.('input') ?? node 167→ } 168→ }} 169→ inputClassName={`tui-input-reset ${props.inputClassName ?? ''}`.trim()} 170→ size={props.size} 171→ type={props.type ?? 'text'} 172→ placeholder={props.placeholder} 173→ readOnly={props.readOnly} 174→ value={inputMask ? getInitialMaskValue() : value} 175→ onChange={event => setValue(event.target.value)} 176→ name={props.name ?? ''} 177→ prefix={prefix} 178→ suffix={suffix} 179→ /> 180→ 181→ {props.description && 182→ 183→ {props.description} 184→ } 185→ 186→
187→ ) 188→}) 189→ 190→/** 191→ * Strip prefix/suffix from a stored value for mask validation. 192→ */ 193→function stripAffixes(value: string, prefix?: string, suffix?: string): string { 194→ let result = value 195→ if (prefix && result.startsWith(prefix)) { 196→ result = result.slice(prefix.length) 197→ } 198→ if (suffix && result.endsWith(suffix)) { 199→ result = result.slice(0, result.length - suffix.length) 200→ } 201→ return result 202→} 203→ 204→export default TextField 205→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. - Line 161-168: The ref callback for TUI TextInput uses `node.querySelector?.('input') ?? node` to get the actual `<input>` element for masking.

According to task 7:
- TUI TextInput ref IS the `<input>` element, so `querySelector` is unnecessary.
- Simplify: `inputMaskRef.current = node`
- Add comment explaining `Field.HelperText` className approach (using `tui-visually-hidden` class because Field.HelperText doesn't expose a `hidden` prop).

The spec says:

- I'll simplify the ref callback to directly assign the node, and add a comment on line 182 explaining why we're using the className approach for hiding the helper text since the component doesn't expose a hidden prop.

                inputMaskRef.current = node.querySelector?.('input') ?? node
              }
            }}

# Implementation Plan

Generated: 2026-03-05
Last updated: 2026-03-05T19:49:37Z (iteration 1)

## Tasks

- [x] 1. Create shared `isDev()` utility at `assets/src/utils/is-dev.ts`
  Context: Shared utility for dev-only warnings. Pattern: `export const isDev = (): boolean => ...` checking `import.meta.env?.DEV` or `process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'`. See `.harness/specs/wrapper-contract.md` for usage pattern.
  Spec refs: wrapper-contract (dev warnings section)
  Done: iteration 1. Created `assets/src/utils/is-dev.ts`. Used `unknown` cast to avoid ImportMeta type mismatch — tsconfig has no vite/import.meta types defined.

- [x] 2. Fix Button.tsx — warning guards + isDev import
  Context: Replace module-level `let hasWarnedAbout*` flags with `useRef` guards inside the component. Replace hand-rolled `isDevBuild` const with `isDev()` import. Add dev warning when `onChange` prop is passed (currently silently destructured to `_onChange`).
  Spec refs: fix-button items 1, 2, 6
  Dependencies: Task 1
  Done: iteration 2. Added useRef/useEffect imports, isDev import. Removed module-level let flags and isDevBuild const. Added hasWarnedSpan, hasWarnedMissingLabel, hasWarnedOnChange useRef guards. All refs placed before early return to satisfy rules of hooks. typecheck and jest failures are pre-existing (no new errors introduced).

- [x] 3. Fix Button.tsx — destructive normalisation + span branch docs + delete dead .jsx
  Context: Move `destructive → danger` theme normalisation before the `changeTag === 'span'` branch split (currently line 202 only normalises inside span branch, but the TUI path at line 253 passes `resolvedTheme` which could be `'destructive'`). Add fragility comment to span branch explaining it manually replicates TUI classes. Delete `Button.jsx` if it exists (superseded by `.tsx`).
  Spec refs: fix-button items 3, 4, 5
  Done: iteration 3. Split `resolvedTheme` into `rawTheme`+normalised `resolvedTheme` (destructive→danger) before branch split. Removed duplicate `themeClass` const in span branch; span now uses `resolvedTheme` directly. Replaced brief inline comment with `@fragile` JSDoc block. Button.jsx deletion skipped (doesn't exist).

- [x] 4. Fix Text.tsx — TypeScript annotations (`FieldsTextProps` interface)
  Context: `TextField` component is untyped (`props => {`). Add `FieldsTextProps` interface covering all used props: `dynamic`, `inputMask`, `prefix`, `suffix`, `value`, `onChange`, `name`, `placeholder`, `readOnly`, `isDisabled`, `isRequired`, `isInvalid`, `error`, `label`, `labelVisuallyHidden`, `description`, `descriptionVisuallyHidden`, `className`, `inputClassName`, `size`, `type`. Type the component signature.
  Spec refs: fix-text item 1
  Done: iteration 4. Added `FieldsTextProps` interface; imported `SizeStandard`/`TextInputType` from `@tangible/ui` for accurate types. Typed `useRef` as `HTMLInputElement | null`. Cast `useTextField` args as `any` (to be cleaned up in task 6). No new typecheck errors introduced.

- [x] 5. Fix Text.tsx — onChange mount-fire bug (mountedRef guard)
  Context: `useEffect(() => { if (props.onChange) props.onChange(value) }, [value])` fires on mount, sending the initial value to the store immediately. Add a `mountedRef` guard: skip the first invocation, only fire onChange on subsequent value changes.
  Spec refs: fix-text item 2
  Dependencies: Task 4
  Done: iteration 5. Added `mountedRef = useRef(false)`; effect sets it true on first run and returns early, so onChange only fires on subsequent value changes.

- [x] 6. Fix Text.tsx — remove useTextField from TUI path + add forwardRef
  Context: `useTextField(props, ref)` is called unconditionally but its return values (`labelProps`, `inputProps`, `descriptionProps`) are only used in the dynamic path. Move the call inside the `if (hasDynamic)` block. Add `forwardRef<HTMLInputElement>` to the component — TUI TextInput forwards its ref to the `<input>` element.
  Spec refs: fix-text items 3, 4
  Dependencies: Task 4
  Done: iteration 6. Extracted `DynamicTextField` internal sub-component that owns `useTextField`, its own state, and the dynamic render path. Main `TextField` wrapped with `forwardRef<HTMLInputElement>`; forwarded ref passed to `TuiTextInput` via callback ref (also handles inputMaskRef). querySelector in ref callback preserved for task 7.

- [ ] 7. Fix Text.tsx — remove querySelector, fix inputMaskRef via TUI ref
  Context: Line 113 uses `node.querySelector?.('input') ?? node` to get the input element for masking. TUI TextInput ref IS the `<input>` element, so `querySelector` is unnecessary. Simplify: `inputMaskRef.current = node`. Add comment explaining `Field.HelperText` className approach (using `tui-visually-hidden` class because Field.HelperText doesn't expose a `hidden` prop).
  Spec refs: fix-text items 5, 6
  Dependencies: Task 6

- [ ] 8. Add/update Jest tests for TUI render paths (Button mapped layouts, Text non-dynamic)
  Context: Existing tests only cover legacy Button path and dynamic Text path. Add tests for: (a) Button with mapped layout (e.g., `layout="primary"`) renders TUI Button, (b) Button with unmapped layout falls back to LegacyButton, (c) Text without `dynamic` prop renders TUI TextInput, (d) Text onChange doesn't fire on mount. Place in `tests/jest/cases/components/`.
  Spec refs: quality gate
  Dependencies: Tasks 2, 3, 5, 6, 7

## Discoveries

- **Control.tsx has the same onChange-on-mount bug** as Text.tsx (useEffect fires on mount). Systemic issue affecting all fields. Don't fix here — broader change needed.
- **`strict: false` in tsconfig** — typecheck catches errors but won't enforce strict nulls. Good enough for migration phase.
- **Text tests only cover dynamic/CodeMirror path** — no existing coverage for the TUI TextInput render path.
- **Button tests only cover legacy path** — mapped layouts that go through TUI have no test coverage.
- **Button.jsx does NOT exist** — only Button.tsx and LegacyButton.tsx. Task 3 can skip the deletion step.

lines: Done: iteration 6. Extracted `DynamicTextField` internal sub-component that owns `useTextField`, its own state, and the dynamic render path. Main `TextField` wrapped with `forwardRef<HTMLInputElement>`; forwarded ref passed to `TuiTextInput` via callback ref (also handles inputMaskRef). querySelector in ref callback preserved for task 7.
-- [ ] 7. Fix Text.tsx — remove querySelector, fix inputMaskRef via TUI ref
+- [x] 7. Fix Text.tsx — remove querySelector, fix inputMaskRef via TUI ref

Context: Line 113 uses `node.querySelector?.('input') ?? node` to get the input element for masking. TUI TextInput ref IS the `<input>` element, so `querySelector` is unnecessary. Simplify: `inputMaskRef.current = node`. Add comment explaining `Field.HelperText` className approach (using `tui-visually-hidden` class because Field.HelperText doesn't expose a `hidden` prop).
+  Done: iteration 7. Removed querySelector from inputMaskRef callback (TUI TextInput forwards ref to <input> directly). Added explanatory comment on Field.HelperText tui-visually-hidden workaround.
- [ ] 8. Add/update Jest tests for TUI render paths (Button mapped layouts, Text non-dynamic)

Context: Existing tests only cover legacy Button path and dynamic Text path. Add tests for: (a) Button with mapped layout (e.g., `layout="primary"`) renders TUI Button, (b) Button with unmapped layout falls back to LegacyButton, (c) Text without `dynamic` prop renders TUI TextInput, (d) Text onChange doesn't fire on mount. Place in `tests/jest/cases/components/`.

lines: +Task: Fix Text.tsx — remove querySelector, fix inputMaskRef via TUI ref

Added comment explaining `tui-visually-hidden` className workaround on Field.HelperText (no `hidden` prop on that component). Typecheck: no new errors in Text.tsx. content.text:
Task 7 is done. All tasks are not yet complete (task 8 remains), so no `.harness/COMPLETE` file is created.

**Summary:** Simplified the `inputMaskRef` callback in Text.tsx — removed `node.querySelector?.('input') ?? node` since TUI `TextInput` forwards its ref directly to the `<input>` element. Added an explanatory comment on the `Field.HelperText` `tui-visually-hidden` className workaround.
